metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the eval-guard contract for behavior-changing rubric workstreams: name the eval(s) (e.g. `rebar prompt eval plan-review-<criterion>`), the pass condition (precision/recall floor or discriminates-X signal analogous to `discriminates_novelty`), and the CI job that must stay green alongside the parity gate.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria state workstreams that 'change reviewer behavior' must be 'eval-guarded,' but no eval contract is defined: what eval(s), what pass condition, and what CI mechanism constitute an acceptable evidence gate. The plan names only the existing 'Prompt/routing CI drift-parity gate' for rubric edits, but that gate checks for prompt-cache drift, not behavioral change in reviewer output. A workstream that adds a detection lexicon or named FP class to a rubric would change what the Pass-1 LLM finder surfaces without triggering a parity-gate failure.
findings.location: Success Criteria line 1: 'eval-guarded where it changes reviewer behavior'; Success Criteria line 4: 'Prompt/routing CI drift-parity gate stays green after rubric edits'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] WS5 must define E6.no_hedges as a named sub-criterion or suppression anchor in plan_review_E6.md, or WS2 must restate the dedup rule in terms of an anchor that WS5 actually exports.
findings.finding: WS2 references 'E6.no_hedges' as a named dedup anchor for same-location suppression, but WS5 (which owns the E6 rubric enrichment) does not define or mention E6.no_hedges as a named sub-criterion or suppression hook — a residual/dangling reference.
findings.location: child 4ff8-8d1d-ac17-4ed2 (WS2), G-7(b) section and AC4; cross-reference to child 5b15-3568-44f4-4e2f (WS5)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to WS5's E6 AC: 'Define a named no_hedges sub-criterion or suppression tag in plan_review_E6.md that WS2 can reference for same-location dedup.' Alternatively, WS2 should define the suppression rule self-contained and only require co-location logic, not a named E6 sub-criterion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC-3 with a measurable completeness criterion (e.g., 'all 10 child stories are linked to this epic and each has status Closed or a documented deferral reason') or remove it if it is purely organizational.
findings.finding: AC-3 ('This epic is the durable home for the DSO-adoption effort') is organizational metadata, not a durable end-state deliverable — it cannot be false before this work and true only because of it; any epic is trivially its own 'home'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion explicitly covering eval guards: specify that each workstream touching reviewer behavior must have an associated eval guard merged before the story is considered done.
findings.finding: The deliverable 'eval-guarded [workstreams] where it changes reviewer behavior' mentioned in SC#1 has no covering Acceptance Criterion — the AC set only tracks story creation, not eval-guard delivery.
findings.location: Success Criteria item 1; Acceptance Criteria section (all three items)

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all 10 adopted workstreams (with their names, scope, and key design decisions) directly in the plan body or in a linked, accessible specification so the plan is self-contained.
findings.finding: The plan's primary deliverable — 10 child stories with design decisions — is not enumerated anywhere in the plan text; the only source is an external document ('~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md') and an undocumented 2026-07 design session. An executing agent cannot create the correct 10 stories without that content.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section 'Adopted Workstreams' that lists each of the 10 stories with a one-line scope statement and the key design decision made for it (e.g., 'W-3: Add named FP classes to criterion rubrics — decision: FP classes live in rubric YAML, not in shared pass prompts').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite Success Criterion 1 to name a specific observable artifact and threshold for 'eval-guarded' (e.g., 'each workstream that alters pass prompts or rubrics has a corresponding eval fixture in /evals/ that passes CI before merge').
findings.finding: Success criterion 1 ('implemented and eval-guarded where it changes reviewer behavior') is effort-framed ('implemented') and uses an undefined qualifier ('eval-guarded where it changes reviewer behavior') with no observable outcome, threshold, or artifact specified.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'implemented and eval-guarded where it changes reviewer behavior' with 'each workstream delivers: (a) its rubric/prompt change merged to main, and (b) for any workstream that alters reviewer output, at least one eval fixture in /evals/<workstream>/ that passes the existing CI eval harness.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the completion bar between SC#1 ('implemented and eval-guarded') and AC#1 ('created') — either elevate AC#1 to require implementation+eval guards, or add a separate AC covering implementation and eval-guard delivery, so both sections describe the same done state.
findings.finding: Success Criteria #1 states workstreams must be 'implemented and eval-guarded,' while Acceptance Criteria #1 requires only that '10 child stories [are] created' — these are different maturity bars. An implementer could close this epic as done when stories are created (satisfying AC) while the SC demands full implementation and eval coverage.
findings.location: Success Criteria item 1 vs Acceptance Criteria item 1
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC#1 with: '10 child stories created AND implemented; each story that changes reviewer behavior has a passing eval guard before merge.' This makes AC#1 match the SC#1 bar exactly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the 10 adopted workstreams (by DSO gap-report id and one-line scope statement) directly in the plan body, so the AC '10 child stories created' is verifiable without the external document.
findings.finding: The plan's primary deliverable — the 10 adopted workstreams — is not enumerated anywhere in the plan text. The vocabulary of what the 10 child stories ARE (which DSO gap items, what rubric/schema changes each makes, what criteria they touch) is entirely externalized to '~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md', a file not included in the plan. This means the Success Criteria 'Each of the 10 adopted workstreams is implemented and eval-guarded' and the Acceptance Criteria 'child stories created capturing the design decisions' are stated against an unresolvable vocabulary from the plan alone.
findings.location: Success Criteria line 1: 'Each of the 10 adopted workstreams (child stories) is implemented and eval-guarded where it changes reviewer behavior'; Acceptance Criteria line 1: '10 child stories created capturing the design decisions from the evaluation session'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the R-1 part 1 cohort field schema: specify its name, type, placement (finding dict / sidecar _slim / coverage), and encoding (e.g. sorted frozenset of criterion ids as a pipe-delimited string) so the implementing child story and fort-wisp-wren's downstream analysis have a concrete contract.
findings.finding: The plan claims 'R-1 part 1 cohort field, shipped here' (in the fort-wisp-wren dependency statement), but the schema for this field — its name, type, placement (on the per-finding dict emitted by pass1_chunk? on _slim in the sidecar? on coverage?), and encoding (sorted criterion-id set as a string, a list, a hash?) — is nowhere defined in the plan. Verified absence: passes.py lines 294-306 show pass1_chunk appends no cohort field; sidecar.py _slim lines 236-264 carry no cohort key. fort-wisp-wren explicitly depends on this field existing before its own design session, so the shape gap blocks a downstream epic.
findings.location: Context (evaluated but NOT in this epic) section: 'R-1 part 2 (blocking-tier context isolation) -> placeholder epic fort-wisp-wren (depends on R-1 part 1 cohort field, shipped here)'; passes.py lines 294-306; sidecar.py lines 236-264
findings.suggested_fix: Add a schema definition to the R-1 part 1 child story: field name `pass1_chunk` (or `chunk_cohort`), type `str`, placement on the `_slim` sidecar finding dict (not the live finding — observability-only, consistent with norm_id precedent), encoding = sorted-criterion-id-set joined with `|` (e.g. `"E2|F1|G5"`). Co-locate the downstream analysis rule ('contamination = finding appears only in chunks where a known-contaminating criterion is a co-resident') with this schema definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] WS2 hedge-detector design decision must explicitly address ADR 0016 / the frozen DET_CHECKS constraint and either rule out exec:DET on that basis or document a formal ADR amendment process.
findings.finding: WS2 considers 'exec:DET' as a candidate implementation path for the hedge detector without acknowledging ADR 0016, which freezes DET_CHECKS at P1-P9 and is explicitly cited by sibling WS4 as a hard constraint. This creates conflicting assumptions between WS2 and WS4 about DET extensibility.
findings.location: child 4ff8-8d1d-ac17-4ed2 (WS2), G-7(b) open design issue section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to WS2's open design issue: 'Note: ADR 0016 freezes DET_CHECKS at P1-P9 (see WS4 constraint). An exec:DET path requires an ADR amendment; absent that, the 1-TURN finder path is the default resolution.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify integration ordering for edits to plan_review_verifier*.md: WS1 and WS6 should land before WS7's sweep, and WS7's eval baseline should be recorded after WS1/WS6 are merged.
findings.finding: Three children (WS1, WS6, WS7) all edit plan_review_verifier*.md with no specified integration ordering among them. WS7's affirmative-framing sweep could overwrite or miss content added by WS1 (new sub-answer question text) and WS6 (blast_radius ratchet prose) if the sweep is applied without awareness of those prior additions.
findings.location: child 80b3-6a4e-8a20-4090 (WS1), child 9b38-c7a4-1abb-4323 (WS6), child 81c9-f5ad-7c54-4623 (WS7) — all editing plan_review_verifier*.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to WS7 that the affirmative-framing sweep over verifier prompts must be applied after WS1 and WS6 have merged, and that the eval baseline for WS7 is recorded at that point.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] WS10 must add an AC to enumerate downstream consumers of coach output format and verify (or explicitly accept) that the deep-link addition does not break them.
findings.finding: WS10 notes that 'downstream consumers of coach output format' must be considered when adding deep-link URLs to Pass-4 coach output, but provides no AC to enumerate those consumers or verify backward compatibility — a consumer-impact gap.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to WS10 ACs: 'Enumerate downstream consumers of Pass-4 coach output (CLI, MCP, CI comment, any integration tests); confirm the deep-link addition is backward-compatible or list accepted breakage. Proof: grep for coach output consumers in the codebase + check integration tests.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or success criterion requiring the guiding architectural decision (adopt DSO content into rubrics; keep shared passes generic; reject DSO mechanisms) to be captured in an ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or named design doc before the epic closes.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — adopt DSO content (detection lexicons, named FP classes, calibration idioms) into rebar criterion rubrics while keeping shared passes generic — but names no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc artifact to capture this decision durably. The referenced gap report (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md) is an input artifact, not a decision record.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] Architectural decision recorded in ADR or CLAUDE.md: DSO content (lexicons, FP classes, calibration idioms) belongs in criterion rubrics; shared passes stay generic; DSO mechanisms (persona committees, hard-gate prose, word-match) are rejected with rationale.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a deterministic in-session check for Success Criterion 2 — for example, a CI lint rule or diff-check that flags any criterion-name or rubric-id strings appearing in shared-pass prompt files, or a named reviewer step in the PR checklist.
findings.finding: Success criterion 2 ('Generic passes stay generic across findings — criterion-specific logic lives in criterion rubrics/evidence, not in the shared passes') is a structural invariant, not a measurable outcome; no deterministic in-session check or artifact is specified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit section anchors or ownership boundaries in docs/plan-review-gate.md for WS8 (move table, ~lines 148-160) and WS10 (R-3 invariant section), and note in WS10 that it must not reformat the WS8 move table.
findings.finding: Both WS8 and WS10 edit docs/plan-review-gate.md (WS8 updates the move table; WS10 adds the R-3 asymmetric-error invariant and tuning checklist), with no explicit handshake on section ownership or non-overlap. WS10 is sequenced after WS1-8, but the shared-doc risk is unacknowledged.
findings.suggested_fix: In WS10, add: 'Do not modify the MOVE_REGISTRY mirror section (lines ~148-160, owned by WS8); insert R-3 content in a new dedicated section.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Standardize on a single term ('child stories' or 'workstreams') throughout SC and AC, and if one workstream can produce multiple stories, state the expected count explicitly.
findings.finding: The same concept is called '10 adopted workstreams' in the Success Criteria and '10 child stories' in the Acceptance Criteria — minor terminology drift that could cause confusion about whether these refer to identical sets.
findings.location: Success Criteria item 1 ('10 adopted workstreams (child stories)'); Acceptance Criteria item 1 ('10 child stories')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism tied to user-observable improvement (e.g. a before/after false-positive rate comparison on a held-out eval set, a named metric target for reviewer accuracy, or a dogfooding checkpoint with plan authors) and explicitly name the primary beneficiary (e.g. 'engineers submitting plans to the rebar gate').
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism for user-observable improvement is named. The Success Criteria reference internal gates ('eval-guarded where it changes reviewer behavior', 'CI drift-parity gate stays green') but no before/after comparison, operational metric (e.g. false-positive rate, reviewer accuracy delta), or dogfooding signal is specified. Additionally, the beneficiary (plan authors / engineers receiving gate feedback) is implied but never explicitly named.
findings.suggested_fix: In Success Criteria, add: 'Eval set FP/FN delta: rubric changes must show ≤ X% false-positive rate on the held-out plan eval corpus (before vs. after per workstream), reviewed by [named role, e.g. plan authors / gate maintainers] before merge.' This gives a user-observable signal and names the beneficiary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ensure grumpy-gland-rose, only-crave-art, and spin-cub-usher are explicitly linked in this epic (or in a designated child/task) so AC2 is fully satisfied.
findings.finding: Parent AC2 requires all four related epics (grumpy-gland-rose, only-crave-art, fort-wisp-wren, spin-cub-usher) be linked; only fort-wisp-wren appears in any child (WS9). The other three — grumpy-gland-rose, only-crave-art, and spin-cub-usher — are never referenced by any child story, leaving this parent acceptance criterion partially uncovered.
findings.location: child 8656-92dc-f6eb-4a8f (WS9) mentions fort-wisp-wren; no child references grumpy-gland-rose, only-crave-art, or spin-cub-usher
findings.suggested_fix: Add a lightweight task (or a note in WS10, the docs/housekeeping child) requiring that the parent epic carry explicit links to grumpy-gland-rose, only-crave-art, and spin-cub-usher before the epic is closed.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
